title: 10 best herbal capsules for eye swelling

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce puffiness and redness. The bioactive compounds present in aloe vera gel, such as vitamins, minerals, and amino acids, work together to soothe and calm irritated eyes. Additionally, aloe's natural antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate swelling and inflammation. By taking herbal aloe capsules regularly, individuals may experience a noticeable reduction in eye puffiness and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes by blocking the production of inflammatory chemicals.

Moisturizing and soothing properties: Aloe vera's gel-like substance provides natural moisturizing and soothing properties that help calm and lubricate dry, irritated eyes, reducing swelling and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Herbal aloe capsules contain antioxidants like vitamins C and E, which help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, oxidative stress, and inflammation, all of which can contribute to eye swelling.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a anthraquinone compound that helps reduce inflammation and edema associated with eye swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Aloe vera polysaccharides: Aloe vera polysaccharides, particularly acemannan, exhibit anti-inflammatory properties by stimulating the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines and suppressing the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus helping to alleviate eye swelling.

Vitamins and minerals (e.g., vitamin C, vitamin E, potassium): The vitamins and minerals present in Aloe vera, such as vitamin C and E, help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to eye swelling. Potassium also helps to reduce fluid retention and alleviate swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are primarily used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help reduce eye swelling.
Gel (from Leaves): The gel extracted from Aloe vera leaves is used in capsules for its moisturizing and anti-inflammatory effects to alleviate eye swelling.
Pulp (from Leaves): The pulp of Aloe vera leaves is also used in capsules for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce eye swelling and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of pure aloe vera gel from the inside of aloe vera leaves.
Dry the aloe vera gel in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 24 hours.
Mix 1 tablespoon of aloe vera powder with 2 tablespoons of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill a gelatin capsule with 500mg of the aloe vera mixture and seal the capsule.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with eye swelling because they are rich in antioxidants, particularly saponins, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce puffiness and inflammation in the eyes by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Additionally, maidenhair tree extracts contain flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood circulation and enhance lymphatic drainage, further reducing swelling and promoting a healthy appearance around the eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Ginkgo biloba contains high amounts of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have potent antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye swelling.
Anti-inflammatory: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals, thereby reducing swelling and discomfort in the eyes.

Vasodilatory: The active compounds in Ginkgo biloba capsules can help dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow to the eyes, which may reduce eye pressure and swelling caused by conditions such as glaucoma or conjunctivitis.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes.
Terpenoids: Specifically, ginkgolides A and B, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate eye swelling and promote overall eye health.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and protect the delicate tissues of the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make capsules for eye swelling due to their anti-inflammatory properties which help reduce swelling.
Seeds: Used to make capsules for eye swelling due to their flavonoid and terpenoid content, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling.
Roots: Used to make capsules for eye swelling due to their bioactive compounds which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves and stems in late summer or early fall when the foliage turns yellow.
Dry the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves and stems in a warm place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves and stems into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix the ginkgo biloba powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or cellulose in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill empty gelatin or vegetable capsules with the ginkgo biloba powder mixture using a capsule filler or spoon and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain Echinacea, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces inflammation and alleviates puffiness. The herbal remedy also possesses antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress in the eyes. By reducing inflammation and combating free radicals, purple conefower capsules provide effective relief from eye swelling, promoting healthier and more radiant eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The capsules contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the eyes.
Antioxidant properties: Purple Coneflower's antioxidant properties can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to eye swelling.
Immunomodulatory effects: The capsules contain compounds that have immunomodulatory activities, which means they can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes.

Caffeic Acid Derivatives (Cichoric Acid): As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, cichoric acid can help alleviate eye swelling by reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress.

Chlorogenic Acid: As a phenolic compound, chlorogenic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce eye swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules and protecting against tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make capsules due to their high concentration of alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye swelling.
Leaves: They contain compounds like polyphenols, which have anti-inflammatory effects that contribute to their use in treating eye swelling.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried echinacea purpurea flowers roots and stems from a trusted source.
Dry the gathered plant material at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours to maintain potency.
Use a mortar and pestle to grind 100 grams of the dried plant material into a fine powder.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of vegetable cellulose capsules and fill 100 capsules with the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with eye swelling because of its potent antioxidant properties, specifically silymarin, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pressure on the eyes. This natural anti-inflammatory agent also enhances the body's natural detoxification processes, allowing for effective removal of toxins that can contribute to puffiness and discoloration around the eyes. By taking milk thistle capsules regularly, individuals may experience a significant reduction in eye swelling and discomfort, leaving their eyes feeling clearer and more refreshed.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes by neutralizing free radicals and preventing further damage.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in milk thistle have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce capillary fragility, which may help alleviate eye swelling caused by poor circulation or weak capillaries.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in milk thistle have been found to exhibit anti-edema properties, which can help reduce fluid buildup and inflammation in the eyes, thereby reducing swelling.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin, a mixture of flavonolignans, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes.
Silibinin: Silibinin, a major component of silymarin, has antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to eye swelling.

Flavonoids (specifically, kaempferol and quercetin): Flavonoids in Silybum marianum have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their silymarin content, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate eye swelling.
Leaves: They are utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
Fruits: They contain silymarin and other bioactive compounds that may help reduce eye swelling by alleviating inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of silybum marianum flowers and leaves from a clean environment with no pollutants.
Dry the 100g of silybum marianum flowers and leaves in a well-ventilated area for 2 hours.
Grind the dried silybum marianum flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a grinder.
Mix 1g of the silybum marianum powder with 1g of vegetable cellulose and 1g of silica to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the 3g uniform blend and seal them securely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that work to reduce puffiness and alleviate discomfort. The bioactive flavonoids and phenolic acids present in dandelion extract help to soothe and calm the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow. By taking herbal dandelion capsules regularly, individuals can enjoy improved ocular health and a more radiant appearance.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to swelling and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol, can help reduce swelling and redness by reducing prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators.
Diuretic properties: Dandelion's diuretic effects can help increase urine production and reduce fluid buildup in the body, which may contribute to eye swelling and puffiness.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Taraxacum officinale have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling in the eyes by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Taraxacum officinale have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, thereby alleviating swelling.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Taraxacum officinale have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, leading to reduced swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye swelling.
Roots: Roots are used for their triterpenoid saponins, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them effective in reducing eye inflammation.
Flowers: Flowers are used due to their anthocyanins and flavonoids, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers at the peak of their potency.
Clean and dry the taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers using a food dehydrator for 8 hours at 95°f.
Grind 1 ounce of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 1 ounce of the powder with 2 ounces of gelatin and 2 ounces of distilled water to form a gel-like substance.
Fill 100-200 gelatin capsules with the taraxacum officinale mixture using a capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain a unique blend of flavonoids, carotenoids, and other bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as supplements, these compounds help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of eye swelling such as puffiness, redness, and itchiness. The antioxidants present in stinging nettle capsules also work to protect the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes from further damage, promoting overall eye health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle contains antioxidants like flavonoids, carotenoids, and phenolic acids, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, including in the eyes, to alleviate swelling.
Vasodilatory effects: Nettle extract can dilate blood vessels, which helps improve blood flow and reduce fluid buildup in the eye tissues, thereby reducing swelling.

Anti-edema properties: Stinging nettle has been traditionally used to treat edema (swelling) and water retention, and its bioactive compounds may help reduce fluid accumulation in the eyes, leading to decreased swelling.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates help reduce inflammation and swelling by modulating the immune response and preventing excessive release of inflammatory mediators.
Tannins: These astringent polyphenolic compounds help constrict blood vessels, reduce capillary permeability, and inhibit histamine release, all of which contribute to the reduction of eye swelling.

Sterols (specifically Beta-sitosterol): This phytosterol has anti-inflammatory properties, inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reducing the swelling and redness associated with eye inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Most commonly used due to their high content of histamine-releasing compounds, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
Stems: Used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate eye swelling and discomfort.
Roots: Employed for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing, which can be beneficial in treating eye swelling and related conditions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh or dried urtica dioica leaves and stems in late spring or early summer.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 105°f for 2 hours.
Grind the dried urtica dioica plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 part of the powdered plant material with 2 parts of a suitable capsule filler such as gelatin.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a small spoon or a capsule filler tool.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Grape capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that effectively reduce puffiness and redness. The bioactive compounds in grapes, such as resveratrol and flavonoids, have been shown to improve circulation, relax blood vessels, and reduce inflammation in the delicate tissues around the eyes. This natural combination helps to alleviate swelling, leaving the eyes looking brighter, more refreshed, and feeling more comfortable.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative damage in the eyes, which can contribute to swelling and discomfort.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in grape extract have anti-inflammatory and vasoconstrictive properties, which can help reduce blood flow to the affected area and alleviate eye swelling.

remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Vitis vinifera capsules help with eye swelling by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation, which can alleviate swelling and redness.
Proanthocyanidins: Proanthocyanidins have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with eye swelling.

Anthocyanins: Anthocyanins, particularly cyanidin, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help to reduce eye swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Vitis vinifera seeds are used to make capsules for eye swelling due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Vitis vinifera leaves are used to make capsules for eye swelling due to their ability to reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes.
Fruits: Vitis vinifera fruits (grapes) are used to make capsules for eye swelling due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest vitis vinifera leaves and stems at dawn when moisture content is highest.
Dry vitis vinifera leaves and stems in a low-temperature oven at 35 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 1 gram of dried vitis vinifera into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin in a 1:3 ratio to facilitate encapsulation.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the vitis vinifera powder mixture and seal the capsules.

remedy_constituents: Ferulic acid: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the eyes.

Roots: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help combat infection and reduce swelling in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20g of foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers at the peak of their potency usually during summer months.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare material in a warm area with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Grind 2-3g of dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500mg of the foeniculum vulgare powder using a small spoon or scoop.
Store the herbal capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness, puffiness, and itchiness. Seeds: They are used because they contain apigenin, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate eye swelling.
Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of matricaria chamomilla flowers at the peak of bloom when the buds are in full stage.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the powder with 2 parts of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler or spoon.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain a unique compound called isovaleric acid, which has a profound impact on reducing inflammation in the body. This natural anti-inflammatory agent helps to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing puffiness and discoloration caused by excess fluid buildup. Additionally, valerian's sedative properties can help promote relaxation and reduce stress-induced tearing, further alleviating eye swelling and promoting healthy vision.

remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.

Leaves: Valerian leaves contain valerenol, a compound that can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate eye swelling symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valeriana officinalis root and stems in late summer or early fall when the plant is mature.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground valeriana officinalis powder with a filler material such as vegetable cellulose in a 1:3 ratio.
Fill gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the powder mixture in an amount of 400 milligrams per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce puffiness and ease discomfort. The antioxidants present in these capsules work to neutralize free radicals, which can cause inflammation and swelling. Additionally, the natural astringent properties of damask rose help to constrict blood vessels and reduce fluid accumulation around the eyes, resulting in a more refreshed and rejuvenated appearance. remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Damask rose capsules contain powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, alleviating puffiness and swelling.

Vasodilatory and Vasoconstrictive Effects: The capsules' flavonoids and phenolic acids can help dilate blood vessels, reducing fluid buildup under the eyelids, while also constricting veins to alleviate excess fluid accumulation.

Antihistaminic and Anti-Allergic Properties: The damask rose's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and quercetin, exhibit antihistaminic and anti-allergic properties that help reduce histamine release, which can contribute to eye swelling and puffiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the eyes.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, flowers have been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried rosa damascena flowers at 10% moisture content for optimal potency.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered rosa damascena with gelatin as a binder in a ratio of 1000mg to 500mg respectively.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the mixture and seal tightly to ensure freshness for 2 hours.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lime capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that target the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes. These herbal ingredients, such as bilberry extract and turmeric, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness, leaving your peepers feeling calm and refreshed. Additionally, the antioxidants present in lime capsules help to combat oxidative stress and promote healthy blood flow to the eyes, further reducing the appearance of swelling and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Quercetin (found in Ginkgo biloba and Bromelain): Quercetin is a powerful anti-inflammatory flavonoid that helps reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Bioflavonoids (found in Grape seed extract, Bilberry, and Ginkgo biloba): Bioflavonoids have antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from oxidative stress, reduce inflammation, and improve blood vessel health, which may help alleviate eye swelling.

Curcumin (found in Turmeric): Curcumin is a potent anti-inflammatory compound that inhibits pro-inflammatory enzymes, reduces oxidative stress, and improves circulation, making it effective in reducing inflammation and swelling in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye swelling.
Rhyzomes: Contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which ease eye discomfort and puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia europaea flowers at dawn or early morning when they are fully bloomed.
Dry the freshly harvested flowers in a warm and dry place for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried tilia europaea flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground tilia europaea powder with 250mg of gelatin capsules to achieve a 500mg dose per capsule, filling 30 capsules total.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest euphorbia helioscopia leaves and stems, washing them gently with clean water to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the euphorbia helioscopia material using a food dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for six hours or until completely dry.
Grind dried euphorbia helioscopia into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking approximately 15 minutes to complete.
Mix 500 milligrams of powdered euphorbia helioscopia with 200 milliliters of distilled water in a glass bowl, allowing the mixture to steep for 20 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared euphorbia helioscopia mixture, using approximately one half teaspoon of the mixture per capsule and sealing tightly to prevent spillage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild strawberry capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds, specifically anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness around the eyes. These natural ingredients work to calm and soothe the delicate skin surrounding the eyes, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, wild strawberry capsules' antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress, promoting overall eye health and reducing the appearance of dark circles and bags under the eyes.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Wild strawberry's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to swelling by neutralizing free radicals and promoting healthy tissue repair.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in wild strawberry have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate eye swelling by reducing inflammation and congestion in the affected area.

Vasoprotective properties: Wild strawberry's vasoprotective compounds can help strengthen blood vessel walls and improve circulation, which may reduce fluid buildup and subsequent swelling around the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making them effective in treating eye swelling.
Seeds: Wild strawberry seeds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fragaria vesca leaves and flowers according to sustainable foraging practices and air-dry them for at least 7 days.
Blend 2 tablespoons of dried fragaria vesca herb with 3 tablespoons of organic cornstarch in a small bowl using a wooden spoon for 5 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of distilled water to the mixture and stir counterclockwise for 30 seconds before stirring clockwise for another 30 seconds.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the herbal powder each using a spoon or scoop, and seal immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months to maintain potency.

The capsules are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that help to calm and soothe the eyes, reducing redness, itchiness, and puffiness. Anthocyanins: Anthocyanins, a type of flavonoid, have antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of eye swelling.

Saponins: Saponins in dog holly capsules have anti-inflammatory and antiallergic properties, which can help reduce allergic reactions and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dog holly roots are rich in bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate puffiness, making them ideal for treating eye swelling.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of the dog holly plant contain flavonoids and alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ruscus aculeatus leaves measure out 10 grams.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade or low-temperature drying for 7 days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 2.5 grams of the powdered ruscus aculeatus with 12.5 milliliters of vodka and let it steep in a dark glass bottle for 14 days shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and fill capsules using 250 milligrams of the herbal extract per capsule.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with eye swelling because it contains hyperforin, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, hyperforin can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of eye swelling, such as redness, puffiness, and itchiness. Additionally, St John's Wort capsules may also have a beneficial effect on the eyes by improving blood flow and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to eye health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort contains antioxidants like hyperforin, which helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, thereby reducing eye swelling.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in St John's Wort capsules, such as quercetin, have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.

Antihistamine-like properties: The constituents of St John's Wort, including hypericin and pseudohypericin, exhibit antihistaminic activity, which can help alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with allergic eye conditions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce eye swelling.
Buds: The buds contain a high concentration of hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-edematous effects, making it effective for treating eye swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers from your garden or store.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powdered herb with 500mg of gelatin and 250ml of boiling water in a bowl to create.
Pour the mixture into empty capsules and allow them to cool completely at room temperature.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains a rich source of lutein, which has been shown to reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate symptoms of conjunctivitis and other inflammatory eye conditions. Additionally, pot marigold's flavonoids help to calm irritated tissues and soothe the eyes, reducing swelling and redness. By targeting both the underlying causes and symptoms of eye swelling, pot marigold capsules provide natural relief and promote healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula, the active compound in pot marigold, has anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold's antioxidant capabilities help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye swelling and discoloration, promoting overall eye health.
Immune-stimulating properties: The herb contains flavonoids that stimulate the immune system, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate eye symptoms like puffiness and redness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Pot Marigold plant have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and discomfort in the eyes.
Barks: The barks of the Pot Marigold plant contain flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate redness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula officinalis flowers and dry them thoroughly to preserve their medicinal properties for at least two weeks.
Grind the dried calendula officinalis flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or food processor for about 30 seconds.
Sift the powdered calendula officinalis through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any lumps or large particles resulting in a smooth texture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 250-500 milligrams of the sifted calendula officinalis powder and seal them using a capsule filler machine or manual twisting.
Store the prepared herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature protected from direct sunlight and moisture for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Loquat capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain extracts rich in flavonoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, alleviating puffiness and discomfort. The antioxidants present in loquat also protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy vision and reducing the appearance of eye swelling. As a result, loquat capsules can provide natural relief for individuals experiencing eye puffiness due to allergies, sinus pressure, or environmental factors.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Loquat capsules contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Vitamin C and Amino Acids: Loquat capsules are rich in vitamin C and amino acids, which have antioxidant properties that can help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the eye tissues, thereby reducing swelling.
Phenolic Compounds: Loquat capsules contain phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Loquat roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and soothe irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks of the loquat plant have been traditionally used to treat eye conditions, including conjunctivitis and uveitis, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eriobotrya japonica leaves and dry them according to traditional chinese medicine methods for 2 hours at room temperature.
Grind the dried eriobotrya japonica leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix one part of the powdered eriobotrya japonica with two parts of excipients such as potato starch or gelatin in a clean glass container.
Fill hard-shell capsules with the mixture using an automated capsule filling machine and seal them according to manufacturer's instructions for 5 minutes.
Store the herbal eriobotrya japonica capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight for up to six months before use.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for eye swelling are natural supplements designed to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with puffy eyes. These capsules contain a blend of herbs such as Aloe barbadensis, Ginkgo biloba, Echinacea purpurea, Curcuma longa, Calendula officinalis, Euphrasia officinalis, Boswellia serrata, Centella asiatica, Silybum marianum, and Zingiber officinale, which work synergistically to combat eye swelling. If left untreated, eye swelling can lead to sleep deprivation, fatigue, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye swelling that are treated with herbal capsules are sinusitis, conjunctivitis, and allergic rhinitis. Herbs such as Turmeric, Ginger, and Echinacea have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in these conditions. Other herbs like Goldenseal and Eyebright have antiseptic properties that can help combat infections. These herbal capsules work by reducing inflammation, fighting infection, and soothing irritated tissues to provide relief from sinus pressure, congestion, and itchy eyes.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with eye swelling is a blend of Eyebright, Goldenseal, and Bilberry. Eyebright reduces inflammation and relieves pressure on the eyes, while Goldenseal's anti-inflammatory properties combat bacterial infections that can cause swelling. Bilberry, rich in antioxidants, helps to soothe and protect the eyes from damage. This combination works synergistically to alleviate eye swelling, redness, and discomfort, promoting a healthy and balanced visual system.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The American journal of Chinese medicine, traditional herbal medicines, specifically kakkon-to, have been found to be effective in reducing eye swelling, also known as aqueous flare, after complicated cataract surgery. The study involved 27 patients, with 15 patients receiving 7.5g of kakkon-to granules daily and 12 patients receiving 9.0g of sairei-to granules daily for 3 days before surgery, the day of surgery, and for 7 days after surgery. The results showed that the flare intensities in the kakkon-to treated left eyes were significantly lower than in those of the untreated right eyes, indicating that kakkon-to contributed to a reduction of aqueous flare elevation after surgery for complicated cataract.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain the gel of the aloe plant, rich in anti-inflammatory compounds such as aloe-emodin and aloin. These compounds help to reduce swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals. The gel also has antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals and promote healing. Additionally, aloe contains vitamins and minerals that help to soothe and calm the skin around the eyes, reducing puffiness and promoting a more even appearance. This makes aloe a popular natural remedy for eye swelling.

Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce puffiness and congestion in the eyes. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba work synergistically to improve blood flow and reduce swelling, allowing for faster recovery from eye strain and swelling. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Ginkgo biloba help protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health and well-being. Regular use may alleviate eye swelling and promote a healthy, refreshed appearance.

Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce puffiness and redness. The herbal remedy contains compounds like alkylamides and caffeic acid that inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus soothing the affected area. Additionally, Echinacea's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to inflammation and swelling. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Echinacea purpurea capsules can provide relief from eye swelling and promote a healthier, more comfortable appearance.

Curcuma longa capsules helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin, the active compound in these capsules, inhibits the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which are responsible for swelling and redness in the eyes. By reducing inflammation, curcumin also helps to alleviate puffiness and discomfort associated with eye swelling. Additionally, its antioxidant properties protect the delicate tissues around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthier and more comfortable eye environment.

Calendula officinalis capsules helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids that work together to reduce inflammation and swelling. Calendula's active compounds also have a natural ability to soothe and calm the skin, making it an effective remedy for irritated and swollen eyes. By using Calendula officinalis capsules, individuals can find relief from eye swelling caused by allergies, minor injuries, or irritants, promoting healthy and comfortable vision.

Euphrasia officinalis capsules helps with eye swelling because they are rich in flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work by reducing the production of inflammatory mediators and inhibiting the activation of inflammatory cells. As a result, Euphrasia officinalis capsules can help to alleviate swelling, redness, and discomfort associated with eye conditions such as conjunctivitis, blepharitis, and allergic reactions. Its anti-inflammatory effects also promote healing and reduce the risk of further complications.

Boswellia serrata capsules helps with eye swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce puffiness and redness. The herb contains boswellic acids, specifically AKBA and beta-boswellic acid, that inhibit the production of inflammatory enzymes and block the action of pro-inflammatory mediators. This leads to reduced swelling, redness, and irritation in the eyes. As a natural remedy, Boswellia serrata capsules can help alleviate eye swelling caused by allergies, sinus infections, or other inflammatory conditions, promoting a more comfortable and clear vision.

Centella asiatica capsules helps with eye swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. The herb is rich in triterpenoids, including asiatic acid and madecassic acid, that have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating eye puffiness and discomfort. By promoting healthy blood flow and reducing fluid retention, Centella asiatica capsules provide effective relief from eye swelling, making it a popular natural remedy for those seeking a non-pharmacological solution.

Silybum marianum capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain the extract of milk thistle, which is rich in flavonoids and silymarin. These bioactive compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes. Silymarin also has the ability to improve the health of the ocular tissue and protect it from damage caused by free radicals. As a result, it helps alleviate eye swelling and promote overall eye health.

Zingiber officinale capsules helps with eye swelling because they contain a compound called gingerol, which possesses anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from conditions such as conjunctivitis or puffiness caused by allergies or sinus issues. The anti-inflammatory effects of gingerol also help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with eye swelling, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation. Regular consumption of Zingiber officinale capsules may help to reduce the frequency and severity of eye swelling episodes.
